decisions.title: Opt-in lives on `BranchPrefs`, not on a global Manager config.
decisions.detail: Each branch has its own Claude tab; users will want hook visibility on branches where they configured hooks and not on the others. Mirrors the existing per-branch `Model` / `Effort` / `PermissionMode` shape so users don't have to learn a new mental model. Persisted via `tmp/sessions.json`.

decisions.title: Toggle UI lives in the Settings popup.
decisions.detail: The popup already shows the `.claude/settings.json` content (S002), so a checkbox labelled "Show hook events" beside the file viewer is the natural seam â€” the user is already thinking about hooks when they have that modal open. Putting it on the Composer row would crowd a UX surface that's already at parity with mobile breakpoints.

decisions.title: Two envelopes â‡’ one block.
decisions.detail: `hook_started` opens a `kind:"hook"` Block in `Done:false` state; `hook_response` flips `Done:true` and stamps stdout/stderr/exit_code/outcome on the same block (keyed by `hook_id`). This matches Anthropic's `content_block_start` â†’ `content_block_stop` lifecycle so the existing renderer chrome (chevron, summary line, badge) carries over cleanly.

decisions.title: Hook turns are role:`"hook"`.
decisions.detail: Hook events are independent of the assistant turn â€” they fire from the CLI's own machinery, often after the turn that triggered them already ended (PostToolUse). A new `Turn.Role = "hook"` keeps them separate from `assistant`/`tool` for rendering, and adjacent hooks are coalesced into a single hook turn (so PreToolUse + PostToolUse don't fragment the timeline).

decisions.title: Out-of-scope deferred:
decisions.detail: real-time toggle without respawn. The `--include-hook-events` flag is a CLI startup arg â€” there's no in-band control_request to flip it. The PATCH `/prefs` handler triggers a respawn; the user is told this in the toggle hint copy.

decisions.title: `internal/tab/claudeagent/client.go`
decisions.detail: Added `ClientOptions.IncludeHookEvents` and an argv append in `NewClient`. Includes a wire-format reference comment so a future maintainer doesn't have to re-run the CLI to know what fields to expect.

decisions.title: `internal/tab/claudeagent/protocol.go`
decisions.detail: Extended `streamMsg` with `HookID`, `HookName`, `HookEvent`, `HookOutput`, `HookStdout`, `HookStderr`, `HookExitCode`, `HookOutcome`, `HookPayload`. Tags match the CLI's snake_case keys. `HookPayload` uses the field name `modified_payload` per the wire shape (untested in 2.1.123 since echoing hooks don't modify payload, but documented for the PreToolUse-with-rewrite case).

decisions.title: `internal/tab/claudeagent/normalize.go`
decisions.detail: New `processHookStarted` / `processHookResponse` paths off `system/*`. `hook_response` re-emits a fresh `BlockStart` on top of `BlockEnd` so late-joining clients receive the fully-formed completed block. `hookCompletion` struct keeps `Session.CompleteHookBlock`'s signature modest.

decisions.title: `internal/tab/claudeagent/session.go`
decisions.detail: Added `hookBlocks` map (`hookID â†’ (turnID, blockID)` ref) and `hookBlockRef` struct. `OpenHookBlock` reuses the most recent role:`"hook"` turn at the tail when present so consecutive hooks coalesce. `CompleteHookBlock` has a synthetic-creation fallback for the rare case where hook_response lands without a prior hook_started (e.g. WS reconnect mid-hook).

decisions.title: `internal/tab/claudeagent/store.go`
decisions.detail: `BranchPrefs.IncludeHookEvents` field with `omitempty` so existing sessions.json files don't bloat.

decisions.title: `internal/tab/claudeagent/manager.go`
decisions.detail: Plumbing through `agentDeps`, `Agent.includeHookEvents` (mu-guarded), and `EnsureClient`'s ClientOptions. New `Agent.SetIncludeHookEvents` is the single mutation entry point â€” persists the change to sessions.json via `persistPrefs()` and respawns if a client is running.

decisions.title: `internal/tab/claudeagent/handler.go` + `provider.go`
decisions.detail: `GET/PATCH /api/repos/{repoId}/branches/{branchId}/tabs/claude/prefs` endpoints. `branchPrefsView` is a deliberately minimal projection of `BranchPrefs` â€” model/effort/permissionMode already have their own WS frame paths, so we don't expose them via this REST entrypoint to avoid two writers fighting.

decisions.title: `frontend/src/tabs/claude-agent/types.ts`
decisions.detail: Added `'hook'` to `BlockKind`, `'hook'` to `Turn.role`, and a small block of `hook*`-prefixed Block fields mirroring the Go shape.

decisions.title: `frontend/src/tabs/claude-agent/blocks.tsx`
decisions.detail: New `HookBlock` component. Reuses `.toolUse`/`.toolHeader`/`.chevron` chrome with a `.hookBlock` modifier that swaps the accent colour to `fg-muted` so the row reads as ambient automation rather than agent activity. Header includes a tone pip keyed off `hookOutcome` + `hookExitCode`. Body has a meta grid (event / exit / outcome) plus optional stdout / stderr / modified-payload panels.

decisions.title: `frontend/src/tabs/claude-agent/agent-state.ts`
decisions.detail: `block.start` reducer now dedupes by block id when an existing block with that id is already in the turn â€” required because the backend's `processHookResponse` re-emits a `BlockStart` to update the block in place. Default-role for an implicit turn from `block.start` is `'hook'` when the block is a hook block, else `'assistant'`.

decisions.title: `frontend/src/tabs/claude-agent/settings-popup.tsx`
decisions.detail: Added a toggle row above the existing scope cards. Reads `/prefs` on open, PATCHes on click. Hidden gracefully when the prefs endpoint is missing (forward-compat for older binaries that never shipped this).

decisions.title: Go unit tests
decisions.detail: (`go test ./internal/tab/claudeagent/... -run Hook`): - `TestHookEvents_StartedAndResponseProduceHookBlock` â€” full lifecycle (started â†’ response â†’ stamped block). - `TestHookEvents_MultipleHooksInSameTurn` â€” Pre+Post coalesce into one role:"hook" turn. - `TestHookEvents_OptInClientOption` â€” argv contract.

decisions.title: Synthetic-injection E2E
decisions.detail: (`tests/e2e/s005_hook_events.py`): - 12 PASS against `make serve INSTANCE=dev` on port 8241. Verifies REST `/prefs` round-trip, page mount, hook block render, header label, expand â†’ stdout/stderr panels, outcome string, Settings popup toggle visibility.

decisions.title: Live-CLI wire-format check
decisions.detail: (`tests/e2e/s005_hook_cli_wire.py`): - 4 PASS against `claude 2.1.123` with no palmux server in the loop. Spawns the CLI in a temp cwd containing a probe hook, sends one Bash request, asserts the captured `hook_started` / `hook_response` envelopes carry the fields we depend on.

decisions.title: The dev instance and the host palmux2 share `tmp/sessions.json`
decisions.detail: The dev instance and the host palmux2 share `tmp/sessions.json` because both `make serve` invocations cd into the same worktree. We rely on per-branch keying (`{repoId}/{branchId}`) to avoid collisions; works fine in practice for S005 but worth keeping in mind for any future per-instance state.

decisions.title: We have not exercised `modified_payload` (a hook that rewrites the
decisions.detail: We have not exercised `modified_payload` (a hook that rewrites the tool input). The wire field exists in our struct, the UI panel is there, but no live test fires it. PostToolUse hooks that block tool use would also surface as `outcome:"blocked"` â€” `hookTone` already handles that (renders in error tone). Backlog: add a hook fixture that demonstrates payload modification once we have a use case.

decisions.title: The HookBlock auto-collapses on completion via the same falseâ†’true
decisions.detail: The HookBlock auto-collapses on completion via the same falseâ†’true effect the TaskTreeBlock uses. If a user has expanded a finished hook on reload and it gets re-emitted by a fresh `block.start` (e.g. WS reconnect), the auto-collapse would not slam shut because `prevDone.current` is already `true`. Worth a regression test if the reload path turns out to be common.
